questions.title: Two's Complement Negation

hints: Remember that two's complement negation: invert all bits and add 1
Remember that this is how computers represent negative numbers

questions.title: Signed Integer Overflow
questions.description: What is the behavior of this code?
questions.explanation: Signed integer overflow is undefined behavior in C. In practice, on two's complement systems, it typically wraps to INT_MIN (-2147483648), but the standard doesn't guarantee this. Undefined behavior means the C standard doesn't specify what should happen. Avoid it by following language rules carefully.
hints: Remember that iNT_MAX + 1 exceeds the maximum value
Remember that signed overflow is undefined by the C standard
Remember that most systems use two's complement wrapping
questions.hint: Remember that this code exhibits undefined behavior. Consider what C standard says about this operation.

questions.title: Trace GCD Function
